name: Marcus J. Whitfield
summary: Senior healthcare technology executive with 18 years of experience leading product and engineering organizations at growth-stage and enterprise health-tech companies. Deep expertise in EHR integrations, value-based care platforms, and FDA-regulated SaaS. Adept at scaling engineering teams from 12 to 80+ and driving 8–12x revenue growth through product-market fit and strategic partnerships.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Developed HL7 v2 integration modules connecting EMR systems for 14 hospital clients in New England.
Reduced data ingestion errors by 43% through schema validation improvements.
Mentored 2 junior engineers as an informal tech lead.

experience.title: Senior Product Manager
bullets: Owned the product roadmap for a care coordination platform used by 38 ACOs across 11 states.
Launched 4 major product releases that contributed to ARR growth from $4.2M to $19.7M.
Led HIPAA compliance overhaul reducing security audit findings from 27 to 3.
Managed a cross-functional team of 18 including engineers, designers, and clinical informaticists.

experience.title: VP of Product & Engineering
bullets: Built and scaled engineering organization from 12 to 54 engineers across Boston and Hyderabad offices.
Drove product strategy for a value-based care analytics platform; grew customer base from 22 to 190 health systems.
Closed 3 key OEM partnerships with Epic, Cerner, and Athenahealth adding $8.3M in contracted ARR.
Oversaw $21M Series B technical due diligence process; company acquired by NovaCare Group in 2018 for $140M.

experience.title: Chief Product Officer
bullets: Leading product, engineering, and data science for a 210-person business unit with $74M ARR.
Launched AI-driven risk stratification module adopted by 67 health plan clients within 12 months of release.
Reduced platform churn from 18% to 7% through a customer success-driven product iteration program.
Built and chair an external Clinical Advisory Board of 9 physicians to guide product direction.
Managing $38M annual technology budget with consistent delivery within 3% of target.

education.institution: Boston University Questrom School of Business

achievements: HIMSS Stage 7 implementation lead — ClarityPath Health, 2017
Guest lecturer, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health, Digital Health Innovation course (2021–present)
